notes: Group H: Spain, Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia, Uruguay. Group J: Argentina, Algeria, Austria, Jordan. Most likely matchup: Spain (Group H winner) vs Algeria or Austria (Group J runner-up). Spain is -370 to -500 favorite to win Group H. In Group J, Argentina is heavy favorite; Austria (+350) and Algeria (+750) compete for second. H2H and player data below represent Spain vs Algeria as the primary likely matchup, with Austria notes included.
h2h_summary: Spain and Algeria have met only once in official competition, a 3-0 Spain victory in the 1986 FIFA World Cup group stage in Mexico; their head-to-head record strongly favours Spain.

notable_results: Spain 3-0 Algeria - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 1986
Algeria 2-1 West Germany - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 1982 (Algeria's greatest WC upset, not vs Spain)
Algeria 1-2 Germany (AET) - FIFA World Cup Round of 16 - 2014 (Algeria's best WC run)

key_players_home: Rodri (central midfielder, Ballon d'Or 2024, midfield anchor)
Mikel Oyarzabal (striker, 11 goals in last 10 Spain appearances)
Pedri (central midfielder, creative pulse and tempo-setter)
Nico Williams (left winger, pace and direct running)
key_players_away: Riyad Mahrez (captain/winger, 35 years old, 113 caps, 38 international goals)
Mohamed Amine Amoura (striker, 10 qualifying goals, Algeria's most dangerous attacker)
Houssem Aouar (attacking midfielder, creative late runner into scoring positions)
Rayan Ait-Nouri (left back/winger, Manchester City, dynamic overlapping runs)
Ibrahim Maza (midfielder, Bayer Leverkusen, next-generation talent)
team_style_home: Spain play a 4-3-3 (adjustable to 4-2-3-1) built on possession-based high press and rapid transitions, combining patient build-up play with explosive wide threats through Yamal and Williams.
team_style_away: Algeria operate in a 4-4-2 emphasising intense attack-minded combination play with wide runners creating space for central combinations, relying on Mahrez's creativity and Amoura's direct running.
wc_history_home: Spain won their only FIFA World Cup in 2010, defeating the Netherlands 1-0 in extra time, and have appeared at 16 of 22 tournaments with a fourth-place finish in 1950 as their best pre-2010 result.
wc_history_away: Algeria have appeared at four World Cups (1982, 1986, 2010, 2014), with their best run a Round of 16 exit to Germany in 2014, and famously upset West Germany 2-1 in 1982 before being controversially eliminated.
group_context: This Round of 32 tie pits Group H winner (most likely Spain, European champions and 2026 tournament favourites) against the Group J runner-up (most likely Austria under Rangnick's gegenpressing system at +350, or Algeria with Mahrez at +750), with the winner advancing to the Round of 16 and a potential clash with a Group I or Group K qualifier.
strengths_home: Elite midfield control and possession with Rodri and Pedri dictating tempo
World-class wide threat through Lamine Yamal, one of the most dangerous wingers at this tournament
Prolific striker in Oyarzabal arriving in peak form with 11 goals in 10 pre-tournament matches
strengths_away: Lethal counter-attacking pace through Amoura and Ait-Nouri on the flanks
Riyad Mahrez's technical brilliance and big-game experience in tight spaces
Strong qualifying form (dominated CAF with 8 wins from 10, beat Netherlands 1-0 in June 2026 friendly)
